reasoning: Francis Suarez has previously floated running for Florida governor as his mayoral term concludes in November 2025. In early May 2025, when asked about a gubernatorial bid, he said he had "certainly considered it." However, he heavily downplayed his prospects by pointing out that Donald Trump had already "weighed in on the Governor's race" (referencing his endorsement of Rep. Byron Donalds, who announced his bid in February 2025), which makes it "hard" for other Republicans to compete. He stated he may seek "other ways to serve," functioning as a near dismissal of a near-term run. As of the June 5, 2025 knowledge cutoff, there is no evidence of Suarez actively organizing a campaign or setting up an exploratory committee, and only 25 days remain until the July 1, 2025 deadline. Given his recent dampening of expectations, an official announcement before July 1 is highly improbable.
